articles.title: 'HELP ME CREATE' IN GOOGLE VIDS NOW INCLUDES SUPPORT FOR GOOGLE SLIDES
articles.summary: Google Vids' "Help me create" feature now supports Google Slides as reference material for AI-powered video creation. Users can incorporate slides, images, and text from presentations into video drafts by referencing them with the "@" symbol in prompts. The gradual rollout began on September 2 for most Google Workspace plans and Gemini subscribers.

articles.title: LIVE PHOTOS ARE COMING TO WHATSAPP
articles.summary: WhatsApp's latest beta updates now allow motion photos on Android and full Live Photo support on iOS, letting users share images with both movement and audio intact. Cross-platform compatibility ensures Android users see them as motion photos and iOS users as Live Photos, while users retain the option to send them as static images. The feature is currently rolling out gradually to selected testers.

articles.title: LEAKED IMAGES PROVIDE FIRST REAL LOOK AT APPLE'S UPCOMING CROSSBODY STRAP ACCESSORY
articles.summary: Leaks show Apple's upcoming iPhone 17 will include a new crossbody strap accessory in vibrant orange, confirmed by images from leaker Sonny Dickson, matching earlier “TechWoven” case leaks with lanyard holes for attachment. The final product and full color options will likely be revealed at Apple's iPhone 17 event today.

articles.title: PRODUCT TRIOS ARE COLLAPSING. HERE'S HOW DESIGNERS CAN SMOOTH THEM OVER
articles.summary: Product trios are increasingly dysfunctional, with designers often supporting multiple PMs or engineers due to resource constraints and unclear design value. Designers can repair these imbalanced teams by becoming strategic translators who bridge gaps between metrics-focused PMs and feasibility-focused engineers, framing design requests through business impact using techniques like "so I can" statements. They should also position themselves as risk reducers by validating user desirability before costly development begins, and understand teammates' individual motivators to craft more compelling arguments for design's strategic value.

articles.title: THE CASCADE EFFECT IN CONTEXT-BASED DESIGN SYSTEMS
articles.summary: Context-based design systems cascade structured meaning and intent through each workflow layer, not just visual values like traditional systems. When design decisions carry context about purpose and reasoning, each downstream phase becomes more intelligent and capable of making correct decisions. This cascading approach enables AI-powered tools and non-developers to participate meaningfully in creation while maintaining system integrity through inherited context.

articles.title: WHAT DIGITAL UX CAN LEARN FROM THE PHYSICAL MEDIA REVIVAL
articles.summary: UX designers can learn from physical media to make digital streaming more human and satisfying by reducing choice, adding constraints, limiting personalization, increasing customization and curation, and even offering retro emulations. While digital convenience dominates, reflecting on the tactile, deliberate, and curated experience of physical formats can improve engagement and user satisfaction without fully abandoning streaming's speed and flexibility.

articles.title: DISCOVER PREMIUM UI SECTION TEMPLATES
articles.summary: This library features modular UI sections for Webflow, Figma, and Framer crafted by designers who care about detail, hierarchy, and soul.

articles.title: YOUR WORDS TO MOBILE APPS
articles.summary: CatDoes turns your ideas into fully functional apps without writing a single line of code. Just tell it what you need, and watch AI bring it to life.

articles.title: 90'S CURSOR EFFECTS
articles.summary: Go back to the wonderful days when knowing how to get your little mouse arrow to dance and sway was most of your worries. Each of the cursor effects below is built for your modern browser and is super easy to add to your site.

articles.title: FIGMA: HOW TO OPT OUT OF AI FEATURES?
articles.summary: Figma automatically enrolled all users except enterprise customers into AI training using their design files without notification or consent, sparking widespread backlash from the design community. The company employs dark patterns by burying the opt-out setting deep within team settings rather than easily accessible account preferences. This incident reflects a broader industry trend where companies prioritize data collection over user consent, following similar controversies like Adobe's recent AI training policies.

articles.title: CREATIVES SHARE TRICKS FOR TAMING IMPOSTER SYNDROME THAT ACTUALLY WORK
articles.summary: Imposter syndrome is common among creatives at all levels, but it can be managed through a mix of practical strategies, community support, and mindset shifts. Techniques include documenting achievements, naming your inner critic, starting projects with small actions, focusing on facts or checklists, practicing self-compassion, and remembering that everyone experiences doubt. Acknowledging these feelings and sharing strategies helps reduce their power.

articles.title: HOW DO YOU REBRAND ONE OF EUROPE'S LARGEST FOOTBALL CLUBS? SMÖRGÅSBORD STUDIO SHARES ITS WISDOM
articles.summary: Smörgåsbord Studio rebranded AFC Ajax for its 125th anniversary, creating a comprehensive visual system that honors the club's heritage while modernizing its identity. The project included a bespoke 15-weight typeface, updated graphics, color palettes, icons, digital templates, and jersey designs, all designed to be flexible across contexts and reflect Ajax's “Total Football” philosophy through a unified, adaptive “Total Design” system.

articles.title: NIKE RISKS EVERYTHING WITH BOLD 'WHY DO IT?' CAMPAIGN
articles.summary: Nike has launched a new "Why do it?" campaign that questions its iconic "Just do it" tagline while keeping the original slogan intact.

articles.title: DESIGN SYSTEM AMBASSADORS–THE GOLDILOCKS OF COLLABORATION
articles.summary: Design system teams struggle with balancing connection and scalability, often cycling through over-engagement, on-demand availability, and complete isolation.

articles.title: APPLE REMINDERS ON MACOS TAHOE INCLUDES THIS INTERESTING PIECE OF VINTAGE UI DESIGN
articles.summary: Despite recent macOS redesigns like Big Sur (2020) and Tahoe (2025), Apple Reminders still features an outdated photo picker interface that looks like it dates back to OS X Leopard.
